questions.question: A worker pushes a crate with a force of $50\text{ N}$ across a floor for a distance of $4\text{ m}$. The force is applied in the same direction as the motion. How much work is done?
questions.explanation: $W = F \times d = 50 \times 4 = 200\text{ J}$. Force and displacement are in the same direction, so work is positive.

questions.question: A person pushes against a brick wall with a force of $120\text{ N}$, but the wall does not move. How much work is done?
questions.explanation: $W = F \times d = 120 \times 0 = 0\text{ J}$. No displacement means no work is done.

questions.question: A box slides $3\text{ m}$ across a rough floor. The friction force opposing the motion is $25\text{ N}$. How much work does friction do on the box?
questions.explanation: $W = F \times d = 25 \times 3 = 75\text{ J}$. Since friction opposes motion, work is negative: $-75\text{ J}$.

questions.question: A student lifts a $2\text{ kg}$ textbook vertically upward by $1.5\text{ m}$. What is the work done against gravity? $(g = 9.8\text{ m/s}^2)$
questions.explanation: $F = mg = 2 \times 9.8 = 19.6\text{ N}$. $W = F \times d = 19.6 \times 1.5 = 29.4\text{ J}$. Force and displacement are in the same direction, so work is positive.

questions.question: A crane slowly lowers a $500\text{ kg}$ beam vertically down by $10\text{ m}$. How much work does the crane's lifting force do? $(g = 9.8\text{ m/s}^2)$
questions.explanation: $F = mg = 500 \times 9.8 = 4,900\text{ N}$. $W = 4,900 \times 10 = 49,000\text{ J}$. The lifting force is upward but displacement is downward, so work is negative: $-49,000\text{ J}$.

questions.question: A waiter carries a tray of food with an upward force of $8\text{ N}$ while walking $6\text{ m}$ horizontally across a room. How much work does the upward force do on the tray?
questions.explanation: The force is upward but the displacement is horizontal. There is no displacement in the direction of the force, so $W = 0\text{ J}$.

questions.question: A car accelerates forward. The engine provides a forward force of $3,000\text{ N}$ over a distance of $20\text{ m}$. What type of work is done by the engine?
questions.explanation: $W = 3,000 \times 20 = 60,000\text{ J}$. Force and displacement are in the same direction, so the work is positive.

questions.question: A sled slides to a stop over a distance of $15\text{ m}$. The friction force is $40\text{ N}$ opposing the motion. What type of work is done by friction?
questions.explanation: Friction opposes motion. The force and displacement are in opposite directions, so friction does negative work.

questions.question: Negative work is associated with ___________
options: Force in the opposite direction to displacement
Excessive force in the displacement direction
questions.explanation: Negative work occurs when the force acts in the opposite direction to the displacement, such as friction opposing motion.

questions.question: A child pulls a wagon with a constant force of $35\text{ N}$. The wagon moves $8\text{ m}$ in the direction of the pull. How much work does the child do?
questions.explanation: $W = F \times d = 35 \times 8 = 280\text{ J}$. Force and displacement are in the same direction, so work is positive.

questions.question: A hockey puck slides $12\text{ m}$ across the ice before stopping. The friction force acting against the puck's motion is $1.5\text{ N}$. What is the work done by friction?
questions.explanation: $W = 1.5 \times 12 = 18\text{ J}$. Friction opposes motion, so work is negative: $-18\text{ J}$.

questions.question: A $0.5\text{ kg}$ ball falls freely from a height of $10\text{ m}$. How much work does gravity do on the ball? $(g = 9.8\text{ m/s}^2)$
questions.explanation: $F = mg = 0.5 \times 9.8 = 4.9\text{ N}$. $W = 4.9 \times 10 = 49\text{ J}$. Gravity and displacement are in the same direction, so work is positive.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered identifying and calculating positive, negative, and zero work!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review situations where force opposes motion (negative work) and where force is perpendicular to motion (zero work).
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on when work is negative (force opposite to displacement) vs. zero (no displacement or perpendicular).
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: Work = Force × Distance. Work is positive when force and motion align, negative when they oppose, and zero when there is no displacement.
